sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.23 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Let$F_n$be a free group with basis$\{ x_1, ,x_n \}$, and let$| |$be the length function with respect to this basis. For${ Aut\,}F_n$we put$|\! | |\! |= \{ | (x_1)|, ,| (x_n)| \}$. Is it true that there is a recursive function$f: { N} { N}$with the following property: for any${ Aut}\,F_n$there is a basis$\{ y_1, ,y_k \}$of${ Fix}( )=\{ x (x)=x\}$such that$|y_i| f(|\! | |\! |)$for all$i=1, ,k$?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
